rulings.Case Title: ANDREW SISNEROS VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1021.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 17, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER ANDREW SISNEROS' Motion For Attorneys Fees Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1021.5. Petitioner's Motion for Attorney Fees is DENIED. Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5 exists "to provide some incentive for the plaintiff who acts as a true private attorney general, prosecuting a lawsuit that enforces an important public right and confers a significant benefit, despite the fact that his or her own financial stake in the outcome would not by itself constitute an adequate incentive to litigate." (Satrap v. Pacific Gas & Elec. Co. (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 72, 76.) Petitioner fails to carry his burden to show that his lawsuit vindicated an important public interest or conferred a significant benefit on society or a large class of persons. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 17, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT. GRANTED. Leave to amend a complaint should be liberally granted when no prejudice is shown to the adverse party. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761. Defendant fails to show any prejudice. Defendant's appeal of the Court's denial of his anti-SLAPP motion stayed the matter "on the merits" until July 2, 2015, the date of the remittitur. (See Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 194.) However, Defendant supplies no authority for the proposition that accepting the filing on June 12, 2015 was an action "on the merits" in excess of the Court's jurisdiction. The August 17, 2015 hearing date for this matter is more than 16 court days after July 2, 2015, the date of remittitur. The Court sees no barrier to deciding the motion now. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID HANSON VS. EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Equilon Enterprises Dba Shell Oil Product Us Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 17, 2015, Line 4, Defendant Equilon Enterprises dba Shell Oil Product US' Motion For Summary Judgment. DENIED. There is a genuine issue of material fact as to whether defendant exercised control over the refinery in a manner that affirmatively contributed to plaintiff's injuries. There is also a genuine issue of material fact as to whether Defendant Shell Oil's actual exercise of control over its refinery affected the manner in which Contra Costa Electric conducted its work such that Plaintiff was not "entirely free to do the work in his own way." (Rest.2d Torts, § 414, com. c, p. 388.) "[A] hirer of an independent contractor is not liable to an employee of the contractor merely because the hirer retained control over safety conditions at a worksite, but that a hirer is liable to an employee of a contractor insofar as a hirer's exercise of retained control affirmatively contributed to the employee's injuries." (Hooker v. Dep't of Transp. (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198, 202.) Shell Oil's decision to not de-energize the lines below where plaintiff worked precluded plaintiff from using a man lift. Plaintiff failing to use the man life was a proximate cause of his injuries, which included being part of an electrical "fire ball" that was sixty feet in the air. Since plaintiff's injury, Shell Oil prohibits manual pole climbing. Whether after full discovery and trial a jury would agree with plaintiff that defendant's failure to de-energize the live wires was partly responsible for the resulting accident is not at issue at this point in the proceedings. To properly obtain summary judgment, defendant must show that plaintiff "has not established, and cannot reasonably expect to establish, a prima facie case" of negligent exercise of retained control. (Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 768.) Defendant has not made that showing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NEHUMA GURALNIK VS. QBE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 17, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT QBE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED. An insurer may not be sued for negligent handling of a claim; any recovery must be based on a breach of contract claim instead. (Sanchez v. Lindsey Morden Claims Services, Inc. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 249, 254.) The first cause of action, for negligence, is therefore sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff shall have 10 days' leave to amend the second cause of action, for breach of contract, to either attach or incorporate verbatim the terms of the operative insurance policy, and to plead facts showing the breach, whether Plaintiff performed all conditions of the contract, and how Plaintiff was damaged. (See Oasis West v. Goldman (2011) 51 Cal.4th 811, 821.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MAYA STICKNEY VS. CHRISTY DELASARIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests, Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, August 17, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT HERIBERTO ROBINSON, CHANITA ROBINSON, KRISTY DEL ROSARIO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests, Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Hearing required on sanctions, which the Court notes were only requested against the plaintiff herself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
